reasoning: The latest FRED data shows the AAA corporate yield fell sharply to 4.66% by late February 2025 due to a macroeconomic sell-off and expectations of Fed rate cuts. However, the statistical tool estimates a 0.66 probability of a near-term bounce back, which serves as a highly reliable short-term primary anchor based on historical volatility and recent mean-reverting behavior. In the longer term, the forecast is deeply split: some expect continued Fed cuts to lower yields toward ~4.1% by late 2025, while others foresee inflation and tariffs pushing 10-year Treasury yields up toward 5%. Given the conflicting long-term narratives and explicit instructions to regress distant probabilities toward 0.5, I start near the 0.66 anchor for the closest resolution date and steadily decrease the probabilities towards 0.50 as the forecast horizon extends to 2026.
